revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:E-Gov法令検索]]
'''e-Gov Hōrei Kensaku''' (e-Gov法令検索) is an online legal database provided by the [[Digital Agency (Japan)|Digital Agency]] as part of Japan's [[:en:e-Government|e-Government]] initiatives. It enables users to search and view Japanese [[:en:Laws|Laws]] and [[:en:Regulations|Regulations]] (e.g., {{ill|Constitution of Japan|en|Constitution of Japan|qid=Q237082|lt=Constitution|1=Constitution of Japan}}, [[Act of Parliament (Japan)|laws]], [[Cabinet Orders]], [[Imperial Orders]], [[Ministerial Orders]], [[Cabinet Office Ordinances]], and [[:en:Regulations|Regulations]]). The service was initially operated by the Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications but was transferred to the Digital Agency on September 1, 2021<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20240229013322/https://www.e-gov.go.jp/news/2021-09-02t1914240900_715.html Announcement of the change in e-Gov operational organization (Digital Agency)]</ref>. e-Gov Hōrei Kensaku was officially launched on June 26, 2017<ref name="new">{{Cite web|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/help/#faq|title=Renewed Launch of the e-Gov Legal Database|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications|accessdate=2025-01-05|qq=}}</ref>, succeeding the previous Legal Data Provision System that had been operational since 2001. == Features == e-Gov Hōrei Kensaku allows users to search and view current laws, including the Constitution, laws, Cabinet Orders, and regulations, free of charge and without requiring user registration. It offers XML-based legal data, supporting machine-readable formats for enhanced usability<ref name="data">{{Cite web|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/registdb/|title=Database Registration of Legal Provisions|publisher=e-Gov Hōrei Kensaku|accessdate=2025-01-05|qq=}}</ref>. Additional features include: * APIs and bulk download capabilities for external applications. * Comprehensive legal data, including appendices and standard form tables that were previously unavailable in older systems. * Advanced search options for amended, repealed, or unimplemented laws. == History == The e-Gov Hōrei Kensaku system was launched as part of the e-Government initiative to enhance accessibility and usability of legal information. In 2017, it replaced the outdated Legal Data Provision System<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20171008131349/https://www.e-gov.go.jp/news/egov/2017/news20170928.html Suspension of the Old Legal Data Provision System]</ref>. Major updates in July 2024 introduced a redesigned interface and enhanced search functionality for repealed and unimplemented laws<ref>[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/file/e-Gov法令検索リニューアルのお知らせ202407.pdf Announcement of the e-Gov Legal Search Renewal]</ref>. == Challenges and Improvements == Before 2022, the system faced criticism for delayed updates and incomplete official legal data. This prompted reforms in legal data compilation, shifting responsibilities to the Ministry of Justice<ref name="renew">{{Cite web|url=https://www8.cao.go.jp/kisei-kaikaku/kisei/publication/direct/210702direct01.pdf|title=Improvement of Official Legal Data by the Ministry of Justice|publisher=Cabinet Office|accessdate=2021-07-19|qq=}}</ref>. By leveraging the [[e-LAWS]] system for real-time legal data updates during legislative processes, the system now ensures more timely publication<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.moj.go.jp/hisho/kouhou/hisho08_00215.html|title=Ministerial Press Conference Summary|publisher=Ministry of Justice|accessdate=2021-07-19|qq=}}</ref>. == Limitations == Despite its extensive database, the system excludes: * [[:en:Treaties|Treaties]], [[Notices (law)|notices]], and [[Instructions (law)|instructions]]. * Laws and regulations under legislative or judicial jurisdiction (e.g., {{ill|House of Representatives (Japan)|en|House of Representatives (Japan)|qid=Q659587|lt=House of Representatives Rules|1=House of Representatives (Japan)}}, {{ill|Supreme Court of Japan|en|Supreme Court of Japan|qid=Q1142255|lt=Supreme Court Rules|1=Supreme Court of Japan}}). * Legal texts repealed or rendered obsolete prior to 2017. == External Resources == For legal information outside the database's scope, users can access: * The [[:en:Government Printing Bureau|Government Printing Bureau]]'s {{ill|Kanpō (Japanese government gazette)|en|Kanpō (Japanese government gazette)|qid=Q11452096|lt=Kanpō|1=Kanpō (Japanese government gazette)}} website (paid access). * The Ministry of Foreign Affairs website (selected treaties). * The Supreme Court website (key Supreme Court rules). == Related Articles == * [[Base Registry]] * [[Japan Law Index]] == External Links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/ Official e-Gov Legal Search] {{Digital Agency|qq=}} {{Normdaten|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|E-Gov法令検索|version=103197489|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:E-Gov法令検索]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q48746885}} [[Category:Digital Agency]] [[Category:E-government]] [[Category:Internet properties established in 2001]] [[Category:Internet properties established in 2017]] [[Category:Japanese legal compendia]] [[Category:Online law databases]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
